text.title: decomposition-rails Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decomposition-rails as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Service-object explosion. Every controller action gets its own `Users::CreateService`, `Users::UpdateService`, `Users::DeleteService`; agents copy boilerplate without consolidating.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consolidate variations into a single service with conditional logic for CRUD variants on the same aggregate. Multiple services should represent distinct domain operations, not HTTP verbs.

failure-mode.description: No standard return shape. Some services return the record, some return a Result monad, some raise. Agents inconsistent across files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pick Result or entity + raise, document it in CONTRIBUTING.md, and enforce in code review. Consistency prevents agents from writing fragile error-handling logic.

failure-mode.description: Concerns become dumping grounds. `Searchable`, `Authenticatable`, `Sluggable` end up with cross-concern logic; LLMs can't reason about model behavior without reading every concern.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Concerns should be narrow mixins (1–20 LOC) for single cross-cutting behavior. Complex logic belongs in services or query objects.

failure-mode.description: Form object vs Service object overlap. Both validate; agents pick whichever they saw last.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Form objects validate input shape; services validate business rules. If they overlap, refactor into a single shape contract.

failure-mode.description: Query object drift from `scope`. Same filter exists in both; agents fix one site and forget the other.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reuse scopes in query objects via `relation.active`. Don't duplicate filter logic.

failure-mode.description: Convention not enforced by Rails. `app/services/` is a folder convention only; the next agent introduces `app/lib/services/` or `app/operations/`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a linter that fails CI if files exist outside the canonical folders (`services/`, `queries/`, `forms/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`).

failure-mode.description: Rails callbacks still fire. Decomposing into services doesn't remove `before_save` magic; agents assume "service-only flow" and break audit logs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document when callbacks fire and when they're bypassed. Tag services that skip callbacks with a comment: `# @warning bypasses callbacks`.

failure-mode.description: Pattern collapse under deadline pressure. When asked to "just add an endpoint quickly," agents skip the service layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock convention in CONTRIBUTING.md and reference in every system prompt.

failure-mode.description: Form-object/service-object validation drift. Agent adds a field to the form, forgets the service's internal invariant check.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Centralize: form validates input shape, service validates business rules.

failure-mode.description: Service calling service in same request. Acceptable if explicit; not acceptable if a deep chain spans transactions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force agents to declare orchestration in the docblock.

failure-mode.description: Query objects re-implementing scopes. Agents add `where(active: true)` inside a query object that's already a `scope :active` on the model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reuse scopes in query objects via `relation.active`.

failure-mode.description: Test-per-service ignored. Agents assume the controller spec covers the service; it doesn't cover edge cases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Service skipping callbacks via `update_columns`. Agents reach for it for "performance"; counter caches and audit logs silently break.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document which callbacks exist and when they fire. Flag `update_columns` in code review.

failure-mode.description: Agents invent new layers. "Manager", "Coordinator", "Workflow" classes appear after a refactor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Whitelist namespaces (`services/`, `queries/`, `forms/`, `policies/`, `serializers/`) and block PRs introducing new ones without an ADR.

failure-mode.description: Decomposition without DI. Agents `Users::CreateService.new(params: ...).call` everywhere; mocking is awkward.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Standardize on dependency-injected services or use `dry-system`.

failure-mode.description: Service returns void. Agent emits `def call; @user.save!; end` — downstream code can't compose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always return the canonical entity or Result.

failure-mode.description: Policy bypass via `find` instead of policy scope. Agents `User.find(params[:id])` then check policy; multi-tenant data leaks via timing attack on `find`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Form object inheriting from ActiveRecord. Agents reach for `ActiveType::Object` or similar to "save like a model"; produces ghost columns and confusing schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Serializer leaks fields. Agent uses `attributes :all` or `model.as_json` inside a serializer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
